mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters without the overhead of manual setup. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr command-line interface (CLI) to manage your Vultr resources.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_1.0.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli_1.0.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --name my-k8s-cluster --region lon1 --version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config

```

> This command writes the kubeconfig to your local kubeconfig file

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state

steps.title: Post-installation setup
steps.description:
This step involves setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloud-provider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys the Cluster Autoscaler
> This command deploys the Metrics Server for monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your cluster name

uninstall.title: Remove orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are cleaned up.

```bash

vultr-cli load-balancer delete --all

```

> This command deletes all load balancers

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades the cluster to a newer version.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s upgrade my-k8s-cluster --version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your cluster name
> Verify that all nodes are in the 'Ready' state after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or cluster authentication.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using 'vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy an application or check other namespaces using 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster by adding more nodes using 'vultr-cli k8s scale my-k8s-cluster --node-count 4'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to the following when everything is working: 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION
my-k8s-cluster Ready control-plane 10m v1.30.0'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Vultr CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
